explanation:
The natural language phrase "The system shall implement the following operating modes" implies that the system must always be in exactly one of these specified modes. This translates into two LTL conditions that must hold globally (G):
1.  **Exhaustivity:** At any point in time, the system must be in at least one of the listed modes. This is expressed by the disjunction of all propositions: `(prop_0 | prop_1 | prop_2 | prop_3 | prop_4 | prop_5 | prop_6)`.
2.  **Exclusivity:** At any point in time, the system cannot be in more than one mode simultaneously. This is expressed by ensuring that for every distinct pair of modes (`prop_i` and `prop_j`), it is never the case that both are true. This is represented by the conjunction of all pairwise negations: `(!(prop_0 & prop_1) & !(prop_0 & prop_2) & ... & !(prop_5 & prop_6))`.

Combining these two conditions with a logical AND (`&`) inside a Global (`G`) operator ensures that exactly one of the modes is active at all times.
